comment: Act-1 Swagbound story gate-chain (Signal Town). Maps to the Narrative Flow Map (swagbound-narrative-flowchart-map.md): the public version of Bosch is already moving through town; gates don't ask who Bosch is, they ask which Bosch is processable. Chain: card clique (B7/B8/D8, sets signal:clique_cleared) -> civic proof gate forces the route open (D1/D2/D3, req clique_cleared, sets signal:route_open) -> solid route barrier opens on route_open -> first threshold boss reveals Malady as a symptom and records the first private memory (D4/C6, req route_open). Bosses are milady-derivatives resolved via enemy-overrides (group 448 -> Mifella 2, group 36 -> Returnless King, group 450 -> Malady). All three boss groups (36/448/450) are unescapable (content/battle-rules.json) and their setFlags + once-marker advance ONLY on a win -- a lost boss returns the player to the gate (suppressed until they step away) so the route never opens without beating it. Coordinates are world pixels near the control spawn (2112,1768) and remain tunable to the real clique/gate/threshold spots. Dev: ?flags=signal:route_open to skip ahead.

dialogue: The arcade light flattens Bosch into a better-lit rumor. A clean copy of his face grins from a card nobody remembers printing.
MIFELLA 2: "You're late to your own read. The clique already traded the portable you."
These cards don't start here. They point up the line, toward the yard that prints them.
The gate files Bosch before it ever looks at him.
RETURNLESS KING: "Source. Vessel. Claimant. Derivative. Pick the lie that travels."
Bosch refuses the stamp. The route unlocks anyway -- not because it agreed, because he forced it.
The road north is open enough to see and closed enough to mean something.
A smiling line of route aides keeps checking the same card, never once looking at Bosch.
"The road opens once the gate decides which Bosch is leaving. Please hold."
The first threshold hums with a memory too small to sell.
MALADY: "A private thing is just a public thing with poor distribution. I'm only the symptom you can see."
The card warms, then fails to describe him. Bosch keeps the damaged version -- and the threshold records it.
